behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is module handoff (manifest parser/resolver -> dependency graph -> scanner input). A pure unit test on one parser function can miss dropped transitive deps during wiring. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required, and no AI output is involved. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires extraction/inclusion, not real external API or subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scanner dependency-collection pipeline in-process on fixture manifests/lockfiles containing direct and nested transitive dependencies; assert the vulnerability-check input set contains all expected package@version entries (direct + transitive), with no omissions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily query-construction and response-matching logic across scanner modules and API client contract. A unit test of only one helper can miss range loss at wiring boundaries. Real external network is not strictly required if an in-process fake API verifies request version constraints and returns mixed-range CVEs. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require validating live API/network behavior, only exact range matching semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner -> query builder -> API client against an in-process fake vulnerability service that returns CVEs for same package across multiple version ranges; assert scanner sends exact manifest range and only reports CVEs applicable to that range.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic in one local comparator/sort function. No module wiring, infrastructure, or AI dependency is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sorting function with unsorted CVE fixtures spanning all severities and mixed CVSS values; assert strict order: Critical > High > Medium > Low, and descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at parser/editor/serializer handoff: non-target entries can be unintentionally rewritten when modules are wired. A single-function unit test may miss end-to-end mutation/serialization side effects. No real network/subprocess/AI needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update flow on fixture files (e.g., package.json/requirements.txt) with multiple dependencies; bump one target package and assert only that entry changed in the parsed content and resulting file diff, with all other dependency lines/fields identical.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires creation on a real repository, which crosses real HTTP/API and git/repo boundaries. Mocks can hide auth/permission/API contract/diff publication failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner PR flow against a real test repository and real VCS hosting API credentials; trigger one eligible vulnerability update and assert one PR is opened with a diff touching only the intended dependency version.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic and does not require cross-module wiring or infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call semver comparison logic with representative version pairs (major/minor/patch/prerelease cases) and assert only major increments are flagged as breaking.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This tests orchestration across decision logic, PR service invocation, and report output. A unit test of semver logic alone cannot catch incorrect handoff (e.g., PR still called or flag not emitted). Real network is unnecessary because the required failure can be observed with in-process wired components and a fake PR client.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner decision pipeline with an update classified as breaking; assert PR creation path is not invoked and scanner output/report contains the required review flag/reason.
